title: Kasivisvesvara Temple, Lakkundi
tagline: Kasivisvesvara Temple, Lakkundi — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: The Kasivisvesvara temple (Kannada: ಕಾಶಿವಿಶ್ವೇಶ್ವರ ದೇವಸ್ಥಾನ), also referred to as the Kavatalesvara, Kashivishveshvara or Kashi Vishvanatha temple of Lakkundi is located in the Gad
overview.description: The Kasivisvesvara temple (Kannada: ಕಾಶಿವಿಶ್ವೇಶ್ವರ ದೇವಸ್ಥಾನ), also referred to as the Kavatalesvara, Kashivishveshvara or Kashi Vishvanatha temple of Lakkundi is located in the Gadag district of Karnataka state, India. It is about 12 kilometres (7.5 mi) from Gadag city, between Hampi and Goa. The Kasivisvesvara temple is one of the best illustrations of fully developed Kalyana Chalukya style of Hindu architecture. The Kasivisvesvara temple has two sanctums facing each other and sharing a mandapa.

topPlaces.name: Nannesvara Temple, Lakkundi
topPlaces.description: The Nannesvara Temple, also referred to as the Nanneshvara temple, is an 11th century Hindu temple in Lakkundi, Gadag district, Karnataka. It is notable for being the earliest known imperial-style temple of the Kalyana Chalukyas, one with significant influences from both North Indian and South Indian schools of Hindu architecture.

topPlaces.name: Lakkundi
topPlaces.description: Lakkundi, also referred to as Lokkugundi, was a major city before the 14th century and is now a village in the Gadag District of Karnataka, India. By the 10th century, it was already a major economic and commercial centre, hosting mint operations for South India.

topPlaces.name: Brahma Jinalaya, Lakkundi
topPlaces.description: The Brahma Jinalaya, sometimes called as the Greater Jain Temple of Lakkundi, is an early 11th-century Neminatha temple in Lakkundi, Gadag District of Karnataka state, India. The temple is attributed to Attiyabbe (Danacintamani Attimbbe), the wife of the local governor Dandanayaka Nagadeva.

topPlaces.name: Papanashi
topPlaces.description: Papanashi is a village in the Gadag district of the Indian state of Karnataka. Papanashi is famous for the ancient Kalmeshwara Temple located in the village.

topPlaces.name: Adavisomapur, Gadag
topPlaces.description: Adavisomapur (Gadag) is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Gadag taluk of Gadag district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Harlapur, Gadag
topPlaces.description: Harlapur is a village in the Gadag district of Karnataka State in India.

topPlaces.name: Kanaginahal
topPlaces.description: Kanaginahal is a village in the Gadag district of Karnataka State in India. The first co-operative society of India was registered in 1905 at Kanaginahal village.

items.note: The Nannesvara Temple, also referred to as the Nanneshvara temple, is an 11th century Hindu temple in Lakkundi, Gadag district, Karnataka. It is notable for being the earliest known imperial-style temple of the Kalyana Chalukyas, one with significant influences from both North Indian and South Indian schools of Hindu architecture.

Lakkundi, also referred to as Lokkugundi, was a major city before the 14th century and is now a village in the Gadag District of Karnataka, India. By the 10th century, it was already a major economic and commercial centre, hosting mint operations for South India.

The Brahma Jinalaya, sometimes called as the Greater Jain Temple of Lakkundi, is an early 11th-century Neminatha temple in Lakkundi, Gadag District of Karnataka state, India. The temple is attributed to Attiyabbe (Danacintamani Attimbbe), the wife of the local governor Dandanayaka Nagadeva.
Papanashi is a village in the Gadag district of the Indian state of Karnataka. Papanashi is famous for the ancient Kalmeshwara Temple located in the village.
Adavisomapur (Gadag) is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Gadag taluk of Gadag district in Karnataka.
Harlapur is a village in the Gadag district of Karnataka State in India.
Kanaginahal is a village in the Gadag district of Karnataka State in India. The first co-operative society of India was registered in 1905 at Kanaginahal village.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kasivisvesvara Temple, Lakkundi?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kasivisvesvara Temple, Lakkundi is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kasivisvesvara Temple, Lakkundi?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Hubli Airport (68 km) and the nearest railway station is Hubballi Junction (62 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kasivisvesvara Temple, Lakkundi?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kasivisvesvara Temple, Lakkundi at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kasivisvesvara Temple, Lakkundi?
faq.a: Stays in Kasivisvesvara Temple, Lakkundi start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kasivisvesvara Temple, Lakkundi?
faq.a: The top attractions are Nannesvara Temple, Lakkundi, Lakkundi, Brahma Jinalaya, Lakkundi, Papanashi, Adavisomapur, Gadag.
